specializations.name: Software Engineering
specializations.summary: Building software products, applications and systems.
bestFor: Problem Solvers
Logical Thinkers
People who enjoy computers
avoidIf: You dislike sitting in front of a computer
collegeImportance: Top IIT/NIT/Top CS Colleges will help you land better internships, Jobs and Interview opportununities but many successful engineers are self taught and building amazing companies
familyAdvantage: Y'all have better chances of getting a job/internship if your family has connections in the industry but many successful engineers come from non tech families
qualifications: BCA(won't recommend from open university), if open university then do MCA from top university. Some companies don't shortlist BCA from Open University)
MCA(same here, go for the top university, better money, better opportunities)
Self Learning(Depends here if you have strong circle and shitpost on X and insta to showcase your projects)
realityCheck: Most engineers do not earn FAANG salaries
Most of y'all and your peers might leave Software Engineering as a career and start going for MBA for better salary
People with passion for computers and building things will do well in this field
careerGrowth: Entry: Software Engineer / Junior Developer (0-5 YOE)

salary.entry: 2.5-7 LPA. This is avg trust me. Only top tier colleges will help you get more than 15-20. There are some exceptions but yes let's be grounded with reality
salary.mid: 7-30 LPA. After gaining 5-8 YOE you can expect this if you are working at a product based company. CATCH: IF-PRODUCT BASED FUNDED COMPANY/STARTUP.
salary.senior: 50+ LPA. This is for senior engineers with 15+ YOE working at top product based companies or funded startups

dayToDayWork: Coding-Monekying. Now a days jerking while Claude/ChatGPT is coding for you
Meetings where you discuss about the deadlines you might miss and how to come up with garbage excuses
Code Reviews- Peer reviewing your AI Generated code
challenges: Constant learning. You can never take a break and have to gain new skills and understand new technologies every year
Competition is cut throat. India produces 1,500,000+ engineering graduates every year
Don't forget about Crypto, Metaverse, Web3, and now AI. Things keeps on changing you never know
exitOpportunities: Product Management if you hate coding but love building products
specializations.futureOutlook: Good. Software and Computer Industry will always be opportunities for best talent. For people who understand the end user and business

specializations.name: Mechanical Engineering
specializations.summary: Design, manufacturing, maintenance and optimization of machines, engines and industrial systems.
bestFor: People who enjoy Physics and are machinist
People interested in machines and manufacturing
Who wants to build Hard things like Bikes, Car, somethings that Software engineers only imagine to build
avoidIf: You hate factory environments dealing with bluecollar workers
You want a purely desk-based career(AC walla environement)
collegeImportance: Getting into a top college is much better. Try to get into State Level and National level college because they have good resources and well equiped staff
familyAdvantage: If your relatives or parents were Mechanical Engineers they can guide you better than college professors
realityCheck: Most graduates do not work on Formula 1 cars or aircraft engines unnfortunately
Practical skills matter more than college projects
You are not going to build your own bike. (I'd build ngl)
careerGrowth: This field of engineering is a connecting dot between aerospace and heavy manufacturing Industry
Most of the work is automated using CNC machine but design and how to get things done, no one knows it better than mechanical Engineers
If you want you can enter robotics, Aerospace, Space Industry, and many other fields
Most of the Indian engineers go for MBA for better compensation at Automobile companies or switch their field by late 20s
Bright minded people who want to build things will stay as a Mechanical engineers and usually go for entrepreneurship

salary.mid: 8-20 LPA(Yet to meet someone who makes more than 20+lakhs because most of them go for MBA)

dayToDayWork: Designing products or components like using CAD software. Designing parts, assembly or systems.
Engineering calculation and analysis like if you are building something how much load/stress it can handle
Testing and inspecting prototypes like Ather testing their 2-wheelers before shipping
Maintaining records and doucmentation, reducing cost and waste but making sure end user/customer get best quality product
challenges: Many engineers shift to software or management
Factory locations may be out of City centres
Many ambitious folks who are looking for better paycheck might get disappointed

exitOpportunities.Operations: Many of the engineer who go for management roles move up to handling operations of Manufacturing unit or operation in corporate
exitOpportunities.Sales Engineering: Engineer who want to stay in their field but want to build management skill and move up the career ladder start with sales engineering
exitOpportunities.Finance: Many engineers after MBA go for MBA in finance given good maths skills
exitOpportunities.Consulting: Big4 or rare MBB(After MBA or Tier 1 college) hire for different roles

recommendedResources.Core fundamentals: Thermodynamics, Strength of material, fluid mechanics, Machine design, Manufacturing design
recommendedResources.CAD design: Build 3D models and assemblies, Learn solidworks or CATIA

specializations.name: Civil Engineering
specializations.summary: Civil Engineering involves designing, constructing, and maintaining infrastructure such as buildings, roads, bridges, dams, railways, and water systems.
bestFor: People who enjoy construction and infrastructure
Problem solvers
Those comfortable with field work
Individuals interested in large-scale projects
avoidIf: You want a purely desk-based career
collegeImportance: Top colleges provide better industry exposure
realityCheck: Site work is common in early career
Projects often face delays
Work can be physically demanding
Growth is slower than software careers
roadmap: Move into project management or specialization
futureOutlook: Stable demand due to infrastructure development
Smart cities and urbanization create opportunities
Renewable energy projects need civil engineers

specializations.name: Electrical Engineering
specializations.summary: Electrical Engineering focuses on the generation, transmission, distribution, and utilization of electrical power, as well as the design and maintenance of electrical systems, machines, and equipment.
bestFor: Students who enjoy physics and mathematics
People interested in power systems and energy
Problem solvers
Those comfortable working with large-scale infrastructure
avoidIf: You are uncomfortable with safety-critical environments
collegeImportance: Top colleges attract better core recruiters
Industry exposure and labs improve learning
realityCheck: Field work is common in many roles
Safety responsibilities are significant
Power sector projects can be demanding
Continuous technical learning is required
roadmap: Learn power systems and electrical machines
futureOutlook: Strong demand from renewable energy growth
recommendedResource: Fundamentals of Electric Circuits by Alexander & Sadiku

specializations.name: Chemical Engineering
specializations.summary: Chemical Engineering focuses on designing, operating, and optimizing processes that convert raw materials into valuable products such as fuels, chemicals, pharmaceuticals, food products, and consumer goods.
bestFor: Students who enjoy chemistry and physics
Problem solvers
People interested in large-scale industrial processes
Those comfortable with plant environments
avoidIf: You dislike industrial or factory settings
You prefer highly creative design work
collegeImportance: Top colleges provide better access to core companies
realityCheck: Many roles involve plant operations
Shift work may be required
Safety responsibilities are significant
Core jobs can be concentrated in industrial regions
roadmap: Specialize in a sector such as petrochemicals or pharmaceuticals
futureOutlook: Growth in renewable energy and sustainability
Stable opportunities in chemicals and manufacturing
Automation will increase demand for skilled engineers

specializations.name: Electronics Engineering
specializations.summary: Electronics Engineering focuses on designing, developing, testing, and maintaining electronic circuits, devices, communication systems, embedded systems, semiconductors, and consumer electronics.
bestFor: Students who enjoy physics and mathematics
People interested in hardware and technology
Analytical thinkers
Those interested in semiconductors and embedded systems
avoidIf: You want quick learning with minimal theory
collegeImportance: Important for semiconductor and core placements
Top colleges attract leading technology companies
realityCheck: Core electronics jobs can be competitive
Many graduates eventually move into software
Continuous learning is required
Hardware debugging can be time-consuming
roadmap: Gain internship experience in hardware or embedded systems
futureOutlook: Strong demand due to semiconductor growth
Expansion of IoT and smart devices
Excellent long-term prospects in hardware and embedded systems
